title: Cloud Seeding
focus: Scientific weather modification technique and specific seeding agents.
rationale: A highly specific scientific concept with clear factual anchors regarding the chemicals used and the nature of the process.
sectionExcerpt: Scientific weather modification technique involving dispersing agents like silver iodide into moisture-bearing clouds to induce precipitation. Conducted on existing natural clouds and does not create clouds. Seeding Agents: Silver Iodide (AgI), most common material, known for its efficient ice nucleating properties. Other chemicals used are potassium iodide (KI), sulfur dioxide (SO2), frozen carbon dioxide (dry ice).
evidenceBullets: Cloud seeding is conducted on existing natural clouds and does not create new clouds.
Silver Iodide (AgI) is the most common seeding agent due to its efficient ice nucleating properties.
Other chemicals used include potassium iodide, sulfur dioxide, and dry ice.

title: National Air Quality Index (AQI)
focus: AQI categories, ranges, and the specific pollutants monitored.
rationale: Highly examinable topic for UPSC prelims, specifically testing the knowledge of the 8 monitored pollutants and the 6 AQI categories.
sectionExcerpt: AQI categorizes air quality into six ranges: Good (0-50), Satisfactory (51-100), Moderate (101-200), Poor (201-300), Very Poor (300-400), and Severe (>400). Monitored Pollutants: PM10, PM2.5, NO2, SO2, CO, O3, NH3, Pb.
evidenceBullets: AQI has 6 categories ranging from Good to Severe, with Severe defined as an AQI greater than 400.
The index monitors exactly 8 pollutants: PM10, PM2.5, NO2, SO2, CO, O3, NH3, and Pb.

title: Commission for Air Quality Management in NCR and Adjoining Areas (CAQM)
focus: Statutory backing, nodal ministry, jurisdiction, and overriding powers of the CAQM.
rationale: Important statutory body with specific jurisdiction and unique overriding powers over state bodies, making it a prime target for polity and environment questions.
sectionExcerpt: Statutory body under Commission for Air Quality Management in National Capital Region and Adjoining Areas Act 2021. Nodal Ministry: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change. Protects and improves air quality in NCR and Adjoining Areas (Haryana, Punjab, Rajasthan and Uttar Pradesh). Its orders override those of state governments, CPCB, state PCBs, and others in case of conflict. Chairperson must have at least 15 years in environment protection or 25 years in administration.
evidenceBullets: CAQM is a statutory body under the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change.
Its jurisdiction covers the NCR and adjoining areas in Haryana, Punjab, Rajasthan, and Uttar Pradesh.
CAQM orders override those of state governments, CPCB, and state PCBs in case of conflict.
The Chairperson requires at least 15 years of experience in environment protection or 25 years in administration.

title: Swachh Vayu Sarveshan Award 2025 & NCAP
focus: Award categories, winning cities, and National Clean Air Programme (NCAP) targets.
rationale: Combines the awards and the parent program (NCAP) which share the same context, targets, and implementing framework.
sectionExcerpt: Awards announced by MoEFCC recognizing best-performing cities among 130 NCAP cities. Category 1 (>10 lakh) won by Indore; Category 2 (3-10 lakh) by Amravati; Category 3 (<3 lakh) by Dewas. NCAP (launched 2019) targets a reduction in PM10 level up to 40% or achievement of national ambient air quality standards (60 microgram/m3) by 2025-26 from 2019-20 levels. Implemented by CPCB and monitored via the PRANA portal.
evidenceBullets: Indore ranked 1st in Category 1 (Population >10 lakh) of the Swachh Vayu Sarveshan Award 2025.
NCAP targets up to a 40% reduction in PM10 levels by 2025-26 from 2019-20 levels.
NCAP covers 131 non-attainment and Million Plus Cities across 24 States/UTs.
The PRANA portal monitors the implementation of NCAP.

title: Aerosols and Urban Wind Stilling Effect
focus: Types of aerosols, their climate impacts (Twomey Effect), and the creation of Urban Aerosol Clean Islands.
rationale: Core geography and environment concepts linking aerosols to solar dimming, cloud formation, and urban microclimates.
sectionExcerpt: Aerosols are tiny solid or liquid particles suspended in the atmosphere. Primary aerosols are directly emitted (sea spray, dust); secondary are formed from gases through chemical reactions (sulfates). They act as condensation nuclei, causing cloud droplets to be smaller and longer-lived, leading to solar dimming. The Twomey Effect describes how increased anthropogenic aerosols make clouds brighter by creating more tiny droplets. Urban Wind Stilling Effect creates Urban Aerosol Clean Islands where weakened surface winds due to urban structures block long-range dust and pollutant entry, resulting in lower aerosol levels than nearby rural areas.
evidenceBullets: Secondary aerosols are formed from gases through chemical reactions, such as sulfates from industrial emissions.
The Twomey Effect describes how increased anthropogenic aerosols make clouds brighter by creating more tiny droplets that reflect more sunlight.
Aerosols act as condensation nuclei, causing cloud droplets to be smaller and longer-lived.
The Urban Wind Stilling Effect creates Urban Aerosol Clean Islands by acting as an invisible barrier that slows long-range dust and pollutant entry into cities.

title: Polluted River Stretches and Water Quality Monitoring
focus: CPCB's role in monitoring polluted river stretches and the National Water Quality Monitoring Programme.
rationale: A distinct shift to water pollution at the end of the packet; covers CPCB's assessment report and the NWMP.
sectionExcerpt: Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB) released assessment report on Polluted River Stretches for Restoration of Water Quality - 2025. CPCB executes National Water Quality Monitoring Programme (NWMP) with State Pollution Control Boards/Pollution Control Committees and has been identifying the country's Polluted River Stretches since 2009.
evidenceBullets: CPCB released the Polluted River Stretches for Restoration of Water Quality - 2025 assessment report.
CPCB executes the National Water Quality Monitoring Programme (NWMP) in collaboration with State Pollution Control Boards.
